summary: The template is formula-driven: only 1_RAW_CORRELATIONS holds raw numbers, while 3_INDEX_SCORES/4_WINDOW_AGGREGATES/2_FRAME_MARKUP(A:C) are Excel formulas. The generator fills only the raw sheet + markup skeleton + frames and leaves all formulas intact; phantom frame rows are cleared so empty formulas can't poison STDEV/AVERAGE. Proven end-to-end by recomputing with LibreOffice: the index sheet is populated with correct values and z-scores. See problems.md (P1) for the earlier empty-sheet defect and its fix.

criteria.evidence: build_template3_workbook loads the shipped template with openpyxl and writes one row per TRIBE segment into 1_RAW_CORRELATIONS while preserving all sheets/formulas; test asserts 6 sheets present.
1_RAW_CORRELATIONS filled from decoded_terms.csv; test asserts term columns equal the template header order (visual_attention@6, reward@30) with injected r values and phantom rows cleared.

3_INDEX_SCORES formulas are PRESERVED (D4=`=AVERAGE(...)`, L4=`=IFERROR(...z...)`), not overwritten. LibreOffice recalc (raw/recalc_check_libreoffice.py) shows the ATT index scales x1/x2/x3 with the raw r and z_ATT = [-1,0,+1] over exactly the real frames.
2_FRAME_MARKUP keeps its formula-linked frame_id/second/scene and 0 flag skeleton; test asserts A4 formula preserved and E4 flag in (0,None); phantom rows cleared.

4_WINDOW_AGGREGATES formulas left untouched (C4=`=AVERAGE('3_INDEX_SCORES'!D4:D6)`); markup-dependent windows remain AVERAGEIF formulas that stay blank until the manual markup is filled. Test asserts C4 formula preserved.

_embed_frame adds one openpyxl image per segment in the frame_image column via extract_frame_png_bytes; degrades to blank if unavailable. Verified structurally; real ffmpeg embedding requires a video (manual).
_segment_frames aligns words TSV to each segment window [offset, offset+duration] for scene_description; blank when no words TSV. Verified structurally (test runs without a words TSV -> blank).

generate_report calls write_template3_report which writes output_html.with_suffix('.xlsx') in Template_3 format and it is zipped via write_report_zip; HTML unchanged. import marketing_report + presence checks pass.
openpyxl>=3.1,<4 added to requirements.txt; Template_3_with_frames.xlsx staged in git so the clone-based runtime and loader find it.
template3_report.py starts with '# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-'; no secrets written into the workbook; UTF-8 CSV reads.

manual_followups: Run one real video end-to-end; confirm per-segment frames embed in 1_RAW_CORRELATIONS/2_FRAME_MARKUP and scene_description is populated from the hybrid transcript, and that the Template_3 xlsx is inside the report ZIP.
